evidence: README.md contract matrix declares canonical UI contract paths as src/shared/components/ui/contracts/button.tsx and src/shared/components/ui/contracts/cn.ts, but active high-fan-in runtime modules for the same surfaces are src/shared/components/ui/theme/button.tsx (155 importers) and src/shared/components/ui/primitives/cn.ts (139 importers) per holistic_context god_modules.

runtimeError.ts is declared as canonical runtime helper while README also documents deprecated alias lifecycle in a different file (handleError.ts), indicating governance is split between docs-level contracts and runtime entrypoints.

README.md defines governance gates only as command names (test:contracts, test:arch, quality:check) without mapping each gate to specific contract surfaces in this batch (runtimeError.ts, public.ts, supabase/client.ts, button.tsx, cn.ts).

README.md sets a dated removal target (2026-06-30) for compatibility aliases, but runtimeError.ts exports HandleErrorOptions alias with no deprecation metadata or test-visible expiry marker, so date-based enforcement cannot be validated from code contracts.

Contract surfaces are fragmented across ui/theme (button), ui/primitives (cn), shared/lib/errorHandling (runtime), and integrations/supabase (client) while README presents a unified governance-contract model.

holistic_context.structure.coupling_matrix shows strong UI coupling across sibling folders: src/shared/components/ -> src/shared/components/ui/primitives/ (35), src/shared/components/ -> src/shared/components/ui/theme/ (26), and src/shared/components/ui/ -> src/shared/components/ui/primitives/ (36), indicating the current placement creates boundary ambiguity for consumers.

`src/pages/Home/components/panes/PhilosophyPane.tsx` combines media preload/cache state, autoplay orchestration, and multiple large inline render branches for 2/4/7-image layouts in one component body (~781 LOC).

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/VideoShotDisplay.tsx` mixes CRUD mutations, persistence preferences (`localStorage`), mobile selection state, lightbox/modal wiring, and rendering of several sub-areas in one file (~861 LOC).

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/hooks/useTimelineOrchestrator.ts` acts as a broad coordinator for zoom, drag, drop, selection, endpoint editing, structure-video placement, and UI state with a very wide return contract.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts` and `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts` each implement large, manual task-payload assembly pipelines with repeated optional-field/fallback layering and policy decisions embedded in builders.

findings.summary: Documented canonical contract paths diverge from actual high-traffic runtime entrypoints.
evidence: README.md contract matrix marks src/shared/components/ui/contracts/button.tsx and src/shared/components/ui/contracts/cn.ts as canonical.

The assigned runtime modules are src/shared/components/ui/theme/button.tsx and src/shared/components/ui/primitives/cn.ts, each with very high importer counts in packet context, so consumers are centered on non-canonical locations.
findings.suggestion: Choose one boundary as authoritative and enforce it. Either: (a) move implementation ownership to contracts/* and make theme/primitives internal, or (b) update README canonical paths to the true entrypoints and treat contracts/* as pure compatibility shims with explicit deprecation timeline and importer reduction tracking.

findings.summary: Governance test gates are declared, but not traceably bound to specific contract surfaces.
evidence: README.md lists three required gates but does not define which contract files each gate must fail on when changed.
High-impact contract surfaces in this batch are thin wrappers/re-export surfaces, which are especially prone to silent drift without explicit gate-to-surface mapping.
findings.suggestion: Add a governance table in README mapping each gate to owned files and expected assertions (for example: test:contracts must cover runtimeError/supabase client/public type barrel API shape). Add CI checks that diff touched contract files and require the mapped gate(s) to run.

findings.summary: Contract-facing APIs are spread across multiple folders with high cross-folder affinity.
evidence: README presents governance contracts as a coherent surface, but contract entrypoints are split by implementation-style folders (theme/primitives/lib/integrations) rather than a single contract boundary.
Packet coupling metrics show strong UI folder cross-edges (35/26/36), consistent with organizational fragmentation rather than clear ownership seams.
findings.suggestion: Stage reorg plan: 1) Create target folders `src/shared/contracts/ui/`, `src/shared/contracts/runtime/`, `src/shared/contracts/integrations/`. 2) Move `ui/theme/button.tsx` and `ui/primitives/cn.ts` into `contracts/ui/` first, keeping re-export shims at old paths. 3) Move `runtimeError.ts` and `integrations/supabase/client.ts` into matching contracts folders with old-path shims. 4) Update README canonical matrix to new contract roots. 5) Update imports via `desloppify move <src> <dst> --dry-run` then real move, validate with `npm run test:contracts && npm run test:arch && npm run quality:check`, and finish with `rg -n "shared/components/ui/(theme/button|primitives/cn)|shared/lib/errorHandling/runtimeError|integrations/supabase/client" src` to confirm legacy path drain-down.

findings.summary: Large UI components combine orchestration logic and complex rendering branches.
evidence: `PhilosophyPane` handles preload tracking sets, video event wiring, autoplay state transitions, and three distinct layout render paths (2/4/7 image modes) inline.
`VideoShotDisplay` manages mutation calls, delete-confirmation preference persistence, selection synchronization, and modal/lightbox flow alongside rich UI composition.
`ImageGenerationDetails` contains variant configuration, metadata extraction/fallback resolution, formatting, copy behavior, and multiple conditional sections in one render-focused component.
findings.suggestion: Split each component into a container + presentational subcomponents. Move preload/playback/state transitions into dedicated hooks (`usePhilosophyPaneMedia`, `useShotCardController`, `useGenerationDetailsViewModel`) and keep JSX branches focused on display-only props.

findings.summary: Coordinator hooks aggregate too many responsibilities behind single broad interfaces.
evidence: `useTimelineOrchestrator` returns a large multi-section object (`viewport`, `drag`, `selection`, `pending`, `drop`, `computed`, `actions`, `endpoint`, `uiState`, `device`) while also coordinating many side effects internally.
`useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` combines DB delete workflows, query-cache mutation/invalidation, operation-result policy wrapping, and editor callback wiring in a single hook.

`useSubmissionOrchestrator` centralizes multiple submission modes (managed, automated, queue existing, queue like existing, generate+submit), mixing validation, prompt synthesis, and task dispatch behavior.
findings.suggestion: Introduce feature-specific controller hooks with narrower contracts (for example: `useTimelineInteraction`, `useTimelineStructureVideoActions`, `useShotSegmentDeletion`, `usePromptQueueSubmission`) and compose them in thin top-level adapters.

findings.summary: Task payload construction repeats ad-hoc fallback and field-merging patterns.
evidence: `imageGeneration.ts` builds params through multiple bespoke helpers (`buildLorasParam`, `buildReferenceParams`, `buildHiresOverride`) and then manual spread/mapped-field composition.

`individualTravelSegment.ts` uses parallel bespoke builders (`buildSegmentState`, `buildOrchestratorDetails`, `buildTaskParamsPayload`, `buildIndividualSegmentParamsPayload`) with deep fallback chains from params/original/orchestrator snapshots.
Completion orchestration in edge function applies similarly layered policy + persistence decisions, reinforcing multiple independent orchestration styles instead of a shared composition contract.
findings.suggestion: Define a shared task-composition contract for fallback precedence and optional-field inclusion (e.g., reusable `resolveField`, `composeOptionalSegments`, and typed schema validators), then migrate task families to that single assembly pattern.

findings.summary: Boolean/state accumulation in interaction-heavy components increases change risk.
evidence: `VideoShotDisplay` keeps many local flags (`isEditingName`, `isDeleteDialogOpen`, `isVideoModalOpen`, `showVideo`, `isFinalVideoLightboxOpen`, `isSelectedForAddition`, `skipConfirmationChecked`) that interact across handlers.

`useTimelineOrchestrator` tracks multiple interaction flags and refs (`showVideoBrowser`, `isUploadingStructureVideo`, drag/end refs, endpoint drag refs, pending states), creating dense coupling between concerns.
`PhilosophyPane` combines multiple media state sets/refs and synchronization effects that coordinate open/close/video lifecycle behavior.
findings.suggestion: Replace ad-hoc flag growth with explicit reducer/state-machine models per feature area (shot card state, timeline interaction state, philosophy media lifecycle) so transitions are centralized and easier to validate.
